name: Supervisor David Canepa
subject: 2024 November e-newsletter
body:
| | | |
---
| | |
# 2024 November e-newsletter
---
|
---
| **Top of the Hill Festival fosters economic vitality**

Dear friends, Thank you for all who participated in the inaugural and very successful Top of the Hill Festival in Daly City, Saturday, Oct. 19. I couldn’t be prouder of city leadership to bring this vibrant celebration of the city’s economic vitality, which offered a unique platform for the arts, entertainment and community connection. And thank you to my colleagues on the Board of Supervisors for approving my request of $35,000 in Measure K district-discretionary funds to support this incredible community-building festival. As an economic development strategy to support local businesses in the northern segment of the Mission Street Corridor, city leadership believes it is critical to bring in visitors and local residents to the neighborhood to spotlight and invigorate the area. This festival will be catalyst for such an endeavor. By driving foot traffic and creating a lively atmosphere, the city aims to stimulate economic growth and enhance Daly City’s reputation as a thriving destination. Key event features included a vendor market, food trucks and booths, live entertainment, interactive activities, cultural exhibits and resource tables from nonprofit and community organizations. Kudos to Mayor Juslyn Manalo and City Manager Tom Piccolotti for focusing on revitalizing this very vital business corridor in Daly City.
---
| **
Kapwa Kultural Center officially opens its doors** I was so proud to join the Kapwa Kultural Center soft opening at 11-B San Pedro Road in Daly City. It finally has its own home to empower Filipina/x/o youth with innovative programming, leadership skills and mental health education. The proposal for Kapwa Kultural Center formally came about in 2018 and has been a community dream for more than a decade and born out of the San Mateo County Filipino Mental Health Initiative (FMHI) and I was happy to contribute $100,000 in my district discretionary Measure K funds in 2023 to help get the project off the ground. The mission of Kapwa Kultural Center (KKC) is to integrate culturally affirming programming for Filipina/x/o youth that combines leadership skills, workforce preparedness, entrepreneurial mentorship, and ethnic studies to support positive cultural identity formation and mental health education. KKC is a department of the Daly City Partnership, a nonprofit offering social services to promote over well-being. This includes basic safety-net services such as food, clothing, housing (rental assistance), diapers and school supplies. Kudos to Stephanie Balon, LMFT and Christi Morales-Kumasawa, AMFT, both Pinay mental health clinicians, who serve as FMHI SMC Co-Chairs. In 2018, they wrote a Request for Proposal to announce their development of an innovative mental health project in San Mateo County. In due time, the Mental Health Services Act Innovation program approved their social business enterprise model to be carried out in five years with the award of $2.5 million in state funds to carry out the project. This is an incredible accomplishment and I encourage anyone feeling lonely or stressed to pay a visit and experience some real cultural connectivity.
---
|
**Digital access a necessity for all like electricity and water** Thank you to the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors for unanimously passing two resolutions in October that I sponsored to advance digital access for older adults and equal access to high-speed internet for every home, educational institution and business in the county. Across the nation, more than 1 in 5 older adults do not have access to the internet which creates challenges that can lead to social isolation and poor health outcomes. We live in a time where reliable, high-speed internet access is no longer a luxury, it is a necessity just like electricity and water. As 7% of our county’s residents currently live without access to high-speed internet, we must double down on our commitment to bridge the digital divide because if we don’t, existing inequities will only deepen. Here in the heart of Silicon Valley, we’ve made great strides to increase access to older adults and our youth, but I can’t rest until every home, every business and every student has access to high-speed internet and the technology to access it. In 2014, San Mateo County launched its SMC Public WiFi program to help build out this infrastructure and provide modern on ramps to the resources available online. In addition to servicing unserved and underserved communities, SMC Public WiFi supports educational opportunities for students, spurs local economic development and provides greater access to county services. To learn more about the San Mateo County Digital Equity Portal and see a list of free public WiFi hotspots in the county go to: [https://www.smcgov.org/smc-digital-equity-portal](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=52db2efa6c&e=c0babbc643).
---
|

**Woodside Road/Port of Redwood City project secures $105 million federal grant**

In July, I announced the the San Mateo County Transportation Authority (TA) and Redwood City will receive up to $48 million in Regional Measure 3 toll funds from the Metropolitan Transportation Commission for the $300 million project. This initiative will enhance the Port of Redwood City and ease traffic at the Highway 101/Woodside Road interchange.

I’m proud to say the TA recently announced the SR84-US101 Interchange Reimagined Improvement Project will receive an additional $105 million federal grant from the Infrastructure for Rebuilding America program (INFRA) that will help close a funding gap

Securing the $105 million INFRA grant was contingent on the TA and Redwood City first securing the $48 million MTC grant.

The interchange provides access to San Mateo County residential communities in Redwood City, North Fair Oaks, Menlo Park and Woodside while supporting industrial activities around the Port of Redwood City, and thousands of jobs at Seaport Center and Pacific Shores.

As the MTC Legislation Chair, I and fellow Commissioner Gina Papan prioritized these improvements to the full MTC Board of Commissioners June 26, which unanimously approved the request. Kudos to the port’s Executive Director Kristine Zortman and SAMCEDA CEO Rosanne Foust (pictured above) for helping to spearhead this project.

Redwood City and the TA plan to start construction in 2027 on a series of safety improvements that will include more than four miles of new bicycle/pedestrian infrastructure as well as improved access for transit shuttles linking the Redwood City Caltrain station and the new San Francisco Bay Ferry terminal planned for the Port of Redwood City.
---
| **Interns wanted to join our team ** We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ce7f883acf&e=c0babbc643). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org ](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org )
---
| **Battling food insecurity **
Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: **CalFresh:**
Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: [https://www.cdss.ca.gov/food-nutrition/calfresh](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=c04ddc3a53&e=c0babbc643) Contact: (650) 802-6576, [antorres@smcgov.org](mailto:antorres@smcgov.org) **Daly City Partnership:**
DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: [https://www.dcpartnership.org/covid19/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=7e7e4ab64b&e=c0babbc643) **Second Harvest Food Bank:**
Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: [https://www.shfb.org/get-food/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=53fc228041&e=c0babbc643) Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 In friendship,
David J. Canepa
---

|

|

clean_body: | | | | | | | 2024 November e-newsletter | | Top of the Hill Festival fosters economic vitality Dear friends, Thank you for all who participated in the inaugural and very successful Top of the Hill Festival in Daly City, Saturday, Oct. 19. I couldn’t be prouder of city leadership to bring this vibrant celebration of the city’s economic vitality, which offered a unique platform for the arts, entertainment and community connection. And thank you to my colleagues on the Board of Supervisors for approving my request of $35,000 in Measure K district-discretionary funds to support this incredible community-building festival. As an economic development strategy to support local businesses in the northern segment of the Mission Street Corridor, city leadership believes it is critical to bring in visitors and local residents to the neighborhood to spotlight and invigorate the area. This festival will be catalyst for such an endeavor. By driving foot traffic and creating a lively atmosphere, the city aims to stimulate economic growth and enhance Daly City’s reputation as a thriving destination. Key event features included a vendor market, food trucks and booths, live entertainment, interactive activities, cultural exhibits and resource tables from nonprofit and community organizations. Kudos to Mayor Juslyn Manalo and City Manager Tom Piccolotti for focusing on revitalizing this very vital business corridor in Daly City. | Kapwa Kultural Center officially opens its doors I was so proud to join the Kapwa Kultural Center soft opening at 11-B San Pedro Road in Daly City. It finally has its own home to empower Filipina/x/o youth with innovative programming, leadership skills and mental health education. The proposal for Kapwa Kultural Center formally came about in 2018 and has been a community dream for more than a decade and born out of the San Mateo County Filipino Mental Health Initiative (FMHI) and I was happy to contribute $100,000 in my district discretionary Measure K funds in 2023 to help get the project off the ground. The mission of Kapwa Kultural Center (KKC) is to integrate culturally affirming programming for Filipina/x/o youth that combines leadership skills, workforce preparedness, entrepreneurial mentorship, and ethnic studies to support positive cultural identity formation and mental health education. name: Hillsdale College via Reclaiming American Freedom
subject: The key to Lincoln's rhetorical success
body:
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid.

This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=edc21af58ff44ab48a17a13613f98716&_e=SQxUslQbNXWxFAsJ3rpItZQTJoPhajmZb4-Tvnk9S_VJnkO2ZhBvZuKOtkUZCiWyrBfglsoqiub7BDu-6zmG-JmdtXX9PZf9MqwWkgtJKMeDJ4uGmXsMF1bJKaA-rJyAKfnO1-QAcUGDYhMx-4v0MMCqtqM4FgLvK0LGsH6f4RFsFmt-skb4c4Bfla5ah6pNuVImYaxcgPSlIpmWGQcLTDXynaM2bG5nhGr-a8yWCqAn55l4zL9PwJD4wMW7OCm3cTt1yR1vsxWwn8WMmh63agmBr-SQc0tlSh_aZTyF3XL7NJYOvmRj62iYha6PLUGU_KyiE2GuUlT8FJZrrEHrnl9VUTpLv1hS6yzNdbltFA4dK4tF3SOrfVjiGM1rT57lJdO9-WKi4BeWlnLXycU3mVKzerkkPqSXoLRtBwRpJW4%3D)
---
| | Dear fellow American, Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid. While studying law in Springfield, Lincoln explained, he became convinced that the ability to demonstrate the truth of things was essential to the good practice of law. Consequently, Lincoln left his job, retired to his father's cabin, and did not return until he could "give any proposition in the six books of Euclid at sight." By studying Euclid, we improve our ability to reason soundly--plus, in my experience, the study of Euclid can be great fun. That's why Hillsdale College has produced a free online course on the topic **" Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry." ** Through** "Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry,"** you will learn the importance of deductive reasoning as a foundation for pursuing knowledge in all fields of learning, as well as in everyday life.
---
| | **Now, the course is available in a beautiful DVD box set for viewing in your home or for use in a small group.** ** ** **You can receive the DVD box set as a thank-you for your tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty.** Your tax-deductible gift will help promote our free online courses and other educational outreach efforts in this critical time for our nation. And your gift matters even more because... Hillsdale accomplishes all its work while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.
---
| [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=edc21af58ff44ab48a17a13613f98716&_e=SQxUslQbNXWxFAsJ3rpItZQTJoPhajmZb4-Tvnk9S_VJnkO2ZhBvZuKOtkUZCiWyrBfglsoqiub7BDu-6zmG-JmdtXX9PZf9MqwWkgtJKMeDJ4uGmXsMF1bJKaA-rJyAKfnO1-QAcUGDYhMx-4v0MMCqtqM4FgLvK0LGsH6f4RFsFmt-skb4c4Bfla5ah6pNuVImYaxcgPSlIpmWGQcLTDXynaM2bG5nhGr-a8yWCqAn55l4zL9PwJD4wMW7OCm3cTt1yR1vsxWwn8WMmh63ahpg8hXbVt6q3HxjKPXXgD-jVwYSpawNqIqpheYRoWI6WSScF7_GTEBEgwcre8HBGG_0MQfuiRCHjsOKOVgJLe7OOZnAFkAh0Dr0ndghjgzTYB-IJEUzhE8VluIkzy3Gvav1DUN88xavO51G3WphKZE%3D)
---
| | That's why we need the support of friends like you, who understand the importance of education to liberty. Quantities of this special DVD box set are limited, so reserve your copy today. I've included a secure link for you to make your tax-deductible gift below: [_**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/dvd-mathematics-and-logic-course**_](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=edc21af58ff44ab48a17a13613f98716&_e=SQxUslQbNXWxFAsJ3rpItZQTJoPhajmZb4-Tvnk9S_VJnkO2ZhBvZuKOtkUZCiWyrBfglsoqiub7BDu-6zmG-JmdtXX9PZf9MqwWkgtJKMeDJ4uGmXsMF1bJKaA-rJyAKfnO1-QAcUGDYhMx-4v0MMCqtqM4FgLvK0LGsH6f4RFsFmt-skb4c4Bfla5ah6pNuVImYaxcgPSlIpmWGQcLTDXynaM2bG5nhGr-a8yWCqAn55l4zL9PwJD4wMW7OCm3cTt1yR1vsxWwn8WMmh63asR3lNNaq2GngAsV9F05gF-RSy8iycW5g0d6v8b5UAU7pLE-QDsDczGbjUFObjTZjMFRxi_N4B4yLLbAELLxOAwKLwg-Ll6xHx0lmSibO35SxJ9fJXOi4cqZEiAwZn2ffhnoIab9yhLK9Fn4Zpzq-ao%3D) Thank you for partnering with Hillsdale in its mission on behalf of liberty. Warm regards, Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---

Reclaiming American Freedom
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_

| | Peter, our race is still too close to call. Kirsten is neck-and-neck with her extremist Republican opponent — but there are still thousands of lawful ballots that need to be counted. Together, our grassroots campaign overcame millions of dollars from super PACs supporting our opponent. Arizona voters heard Kirsten’s message of reproductive rights, water security, and lower costs, and they showed up to the polls. Now, we’re working hard to make sure every lawful vote is counted correctly. **We'll need your help in this fight too. Help us make sure every single lawful vote is counted — and prepare for a potential recount before a winner is declared.** [**Can you chip in just a little now? Arizona voters heard Kirsten’s message of reproductive rights, water security, and lower costs, and they showed up to the polls. Now, we’re working hard to make sure every lawful vote is counted correctly. We'll need your help in this fight too. Voters in Georgia and across the country, including me, are still processing the results of Tuesday’s election.

This election cycle, Georgia Democrats built a movement across the Peach State — one built on Vice President Kamala Harris’s hopeful vision for a new way forward. Vice President Harris was always the underdog, and we knew this would be a tough fight.

We opened dozens of offices across the state, hired hundreds of paid staff, and consistently kept Georgia consistently in the national conversation. The election results do not reflect the work on the ground.

**To every person who organized, knocked doors, made phone calls, and made their voice heard: THANK YOU!**

Donald Trump is poised to raise costs on working families, further rip away reproductive freedom, fray relationships with our allies, and put Americans’ hard-earned benefits on the chopping block.

Georgians are still rejecting this extreme agenda across the state: we not only defended every Democratic seat in the State Senate, but also flipped two crucial seats in the House, with others still too close to call.

Overall, we increased Democratic turnout in Georgia by 60,000 votes. The momentum remains with Democrats fighting for reproductive freedom, Medicaid expansion, gun safety, and an economy that puts working families first.

**Georgia is still a battleground, and we are ready for the next fight.**

Today I’m reminded of advice gifted to us by my friend, mentor and predecessor, our beloved Congressman John Lewis: _“We used to say that ours is not the struggle of one day, one week, or one year. Ours is not the struggle of one judicial appointment or presidential term. Ours is the struggle of a lifetime, or maybe even many lifetimes, and each one of us in every generation must do our part.**And if we believe in the change we seek, then it is easy to commit to doing all we can, because the responsibility is ours alone to build a better society and a more peaceful world.”**_ Congresswoman Nikema Williams
_Chair, Democratic Party of Georgia_

clean_body: If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = Voters in Georgia and across the country, including me, are still processing the results of Tuesday’s election. This election cycle, Georgia Democrats built a movement across the Peach State — one built on Vice President Kamala Harris’s hopeful vision for a new way forward. Vice President Harris was always the underdog, and we knew this would be a tough fight. We opened dozens of offices across the state, hired hundreds of paid staff, and consistently kept Georgia consistently in the national conversation. The election results do not reflect the work on the ground. To every person who organized, knocked doors, made phone calls, and made their voice heard: THANK YOU! Donald Trump is poised to raise costs on working families, further rip away reproductive freedom, fray relationships with our allies, and put Americans’ hard-earned benefits on the chopping block. Georgians are still rejecting this extreme agenda across the state: we not only defended every Democratic seat in the State Senate, but also flipped two crucial seats in the House, with others still too close to call. Overall, we increased Democratic turnout in Georgia by 60,000 votes. The momentum remains with Democrats fighting for reproductive freedom, Medicaid expansion, gun safety, and an economy that puts working families first. Georgia is still a battleground, and we are ready for the next fight. Today I’m reminded of advice gifted to us by my friend, mentor and predecessor, our beloved Congressman John Lewis: “We used to say that ours is not the struggle of one day, one week, or one year. Ours is not the struggle of one judicial appointment or presidential term. Ours is the struggle of a lifetime, or maybe even many lifetimes, and each one of us in every generation must do our part.And if we believe in the change we seek, then it is easy to commit to doing all we can, because the responsibility is ours alone to build a better society and a more peaceful world.” Congresswoman Nikema Williams Chair, Democratic Party of Georgia | = Help Elect Georgia Democrats. The Democratic Party of Georgia works year-round to elect Democrats who share our values of a strong middle class, integrity in government, and equality and justice for all Georgians. If you'd like to donate to help fund our efforts, please = | = | Georgia Democrats have a long history of fighting for change. It's never been more important for every one of us to use our voices to speak up and be heard. But we rely on grassroots support to fuel our fight. Without you, we’re nothing. name: Lauren Groh-Wargo, Fair Fight
subject: We fought hard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight
body:
A note from Fair Fight |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96748659/511051763/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20241107_f_PostElectionNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZGMyZTVmMi0zNTlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LpPzR3oFykEb7keS69ftf5qMuluhY48VWgaHZPt5hxc=&emci=55e273cd-209d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=ddc2e5f2-359d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=1037277)

| | Friends, We’re holding space for this team. This outcome is not what we wanted for our friends, family, and our pro-voter community. We know folks are working through what it means to see Donald Trump rise to power once more. As the final vote totals continue to roll in, we need to keep this in mind: it looks like some 60 million Americans — who are registered voters — opted not to cast a ballot this year. If we are to create a pro-freedom governing majority in America, this is something we need to examine and understand. Our vote is our voice, and we need to make that clear across party lines. It’s also critical to remember: across the country, there were big wins down the ballot, including state legislative races here in Georgia, and ballot initiative wins for reproductive freedom and other key issues. Those victories help prove what we can achieve when we organize. We went all out, knocking on doors, making phone calls, mobilizing voters, and I could not be more grateful. The fight for voting rights in the states and at the federal level will become central to protecting our freedom as anti-voting forces are emboldened. Fair Fight and this community will have important roles to play. Our work to protect and expand the freedom to vote is more important now than ever. Let me take one more moment to say, thank you, friends, for your incredible support of Fair Fight during this critical year. Thank you to everyone in our community for giving your time, energy, resources, and contributions. Now we take a beat, process, and then chart our path forward together. name: Conservative Liberty Wire
subject: Reason with precision
body:
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid.

This sponsored message from Conservative Liberty Wires is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=67862d136be24ab18d5b4973a0514746&_e=Ma7h3MiMzy3QNOLjwc8MAzEZyChPEtTSvp4X4H_7q4kxjZD-7FyOZHaFWIfQGDVfoFdL-v5JP80VdU3OGwkZkypPDhV2VIPSYA1RqE8IB2Rc53oKCgS7vAKGQCZwqPadecrsSagUXW1JUlLEqXlvgOvUgEvU1LtrWmql0F6_vbZWUwfuywsxcUaxlKI4s7OvWPTmzbtIEUGvjMm-9KsmxaCMvs9QG6C9gBUUpd2Hcaz528Q9FYk6xqGSV_KF8UQQ61vbs8_p_elR-GK-1CajtS6Zu6os6IKzakFfv1IcOwyAhgi5f-wlI0P9ny3hhVKxtspNaGNG5wVKs8rz5w_zUuigDxwzbWo90x6emVIPytH340-6Un1gp_OEr1JgBZH8HtbLsUkYTIb91uF1_1hN0FxYaO1EQc8q_C3FJoz-6kU%3D)

| | Dear fellow American,
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid.
While studying law in Springfield, Lincoln explained, he became convinced that the ability to demonstrate the truth of things was essential to the good practice of law.
Consequently, Lincoln left his job, retired to his father's cabin, and did not return until he could "give any proposition in the six books of Euclid at sight."
By studying Euclid, we improve our ability to reason soundly--plus, in my experience, the study of Euclid can be great fun.
That's why Hillsdale College has produced a free online course on the topic **" Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry." **
Through** "Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry,"** you will learn the importance of deductive reasoning as a foundation for pursuing knowledge in all fields of learning, as well as in everyday life.
---
| | **Now, the course is available in a beautiful DVD box set for viewing in your home or for use in a small group.** ** **
**You can receive the DVD box set as a thank-you for your tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty.**
Your tax-deductible gift will help promote our free online courses and other educational outreach efforts in this critical time for our nation. And your gift matters even more because...
Hillsdale accomplishes all its work while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

name: Dr Raul Garcia for U.S. Senate
subject: THANK YOU
body:
I am so grateful for your support! | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/garciaforwa/postelectionupdate11072024?e=3ed3552a03)
---
|
---
[](https://garciaforwa.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe0c756dc01af594bb0bab517&id=b600ec6a68&e=3ed3552a03)
| | | | |
---
|
---
|
# Thank You for Your Support!
---
|
### Dear Friends:
---
| I want to thank you so much for your support and generosity throughout our campaign. It was the honor of my life to run for U.S. Senate for Washington in this great country, and to have the backing of all of you.
Below is my statement, and a link to a Spokesman-Review story on the final outcome of this race.
Jessica and I will always cherish the relationships with so many wonderful people we’ve had the opportunity to forge during this campaign, and we hope to stay in touch. Please accept our warmest wishes to you and yours.
**Sincerely,**
---
| **Dr Raul Garcia**
---
|
---
| I extend my warmest congratulations to Sen. Maria Cantwell on her re-election, and wish her all the best in her next term.
I also want to thank the people of Washington for giving me the opportunity to run for office, and everyone who so graciously supported me. Throughout this campaign Jessica and I have met amazing people, from such diverse backgrounds, who are passionate about making our state and our country the best they can possibly be. I have learned so much from them, and they have only deepened my love for Washington and the United States of America.
Many of the most profound connections we’ve formed during the campaign have been with those affected by the fentanyl crisis: People who’ve lost family members, former addicts, recovery advocates and concerned citizens. This isn’t a political issue – it’s a human issue – and I will continue to do everything I can to help fight this deadly drug.
The late Dan Evans, our former governor and my friend and mentor, sets us a great example of mutual respect and civility even when we disagree on issues. We are always at our best when we seek unity over division.
To my wife, Jessica, and our children, I could not be more grateful to have a better family. Thank you for all your sacrifice, love, and support. I could not be here without you. You are the reason why I am so passionate about our message.
-Dr Raul Garcia _Nov. 6, 2024_
---
|
---
| [**Maria Cantwell defeats Dr Raul Garcia to win fifth Senate term, Spokesman-Review, Nov. 5, 2024**](https://garciaforwa.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe0c756dc01af594bb0bab517&id=afde054c57&e=3ed3552a03)

body:
We can't afford to lose another Senate seat |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96748950/511052826/2020196845?refcode=WTEFHW40_20241107_RosenCureRS_PL_MO&sourceid=1055426&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NWZmNTYwNy0zNjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hLIEpuZmJGUpJQWsmZuchMF5CiuUrKTFYgVMFDv22ZI=) Here's where things stand, Peter: **Nevada's Senate race is still too close to call —**** which means our work isn't done yet. And we're expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes**.

We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada, they'll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda — including passing a nationwide abortion ban. Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: **[Please, will you split a donation now between my campaign and EMILYs List to show the huge wave of support we need to keep Nevada blue?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96748951/511052828/2020196845?refcode=WTEFHW40_20241107_RosenCureRS_PL_MO&sourceid=1055426&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NWZmNTYwNy0zNjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hLIEpuZmJGUpJQWsmZuchMF5CiuUrKTFYgVMFDv22ZI=)** | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96748952/511052830/2020196845?refcode=WTEFHW40_20241107_RosenCureRS_PL_MO&sourceid=1055426&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NWZmNTYwNy0zNjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hLIEpuZmJGUpJQWsmZuchMF5CiuUrKTFYgVMFDv22ZI=)
---
We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and keep Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day — because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country.

Our work isn't done until every vote in Nevada is counted. And that will take time, and money.

So please, Peter, I'm counting on you to come through in the most pivotal moment: **[Can you split a donation right now to help my campaign get across the finish line?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96748953/511052832/2020196845?refcode=WTEFHW40_20241107_RosenCureRS_PL_MO&sourceid=1055426&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NWZmNTYwNy0zNjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hLIEpuZmJGUpJQWsmZuchMF5CiuUrKTFYgVMFDv22ZI=)**

clean_body: We can't afford to lose another Senate seat | = Here's where things stand, Peter: Nevada's Senate race is still too close to call — which means our work isn't done yet. And we're expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes. We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada, they'll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda — including passing a nationwide abortion ban. Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: = | = We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and keep Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day — because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country. Our work isn't done until every vote in Nevada is counted. And that will take time, and money. So please, Peter, I'm counting on you to come through in the most pivotal moment: = = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you so much, Jacky EMILYs List is a community of over five million members that helps elect Democratic pro-choice women to office. = = Paid for by EMILYs List and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Steve Paul
subject: We will not go back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by One Pennsylvania
body:
Here are two things you can do right now. | [ ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96748999/511053087/-501347211?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4NjZkYjcwLTM3OWQtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=h7O5m3JCgglWvmqlExI0wyc7U7FC7yG1GfVO0MMDRP8=&emci=ca6fd104-889c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=d866db70-379d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=3825287)
---
Friends — I can’t tell you how dismayed I am at the results of the Presidential Election. It’s only because of disenfranchisement of voters, and BILLIONS of dollars of pushing misinformation that the far-right was able to clinch this victory. Amid this national rightward shift, there are bright spots. Nationally our movement is sending two Black women to the Senate and the first trans woman to Congress. In PA we helped staunch the losses, and delivered a much smaller rightward margin than in neighboring states. We won in PA 12 and PA 17. Bob Casey’s Senate seat and control of the PA state house are still in play. We will make certain that every single provisional ballot is counted. The narrow Democratic majority of the Pennsylvania house, and even Bob Casey’s senate seat, may hang in the balance. Our team is tired, but undaunted and ready to knock and talk to every target voter in our community to ensure their vote is counted. But even if we protect those seats, our movement’s losses are deep. **At One PA, we’re clear: We will NOT go back.** We’ll fight tooth and nail to protect the progress of the 20th century — and fight for more. We tried to stop them at the ballot box, and we will stop them through organizing, protest, and co-governance with allies. The good news? We have a plan. **Will you commit to join us in the fightback? Here’s what you can do now:**
## 1) Join our post-election National Call to Protect Communities and Democracy this FRIDAY from 8 to 9pm ET.
In the coming days and weeks, we need Democracy Guardians to stand up for the most vulnerable among us, to fight for our rights, and to protect our democracy.
* Hear live reports from the election’s key geographies.
* Understand where we go from here — whether that’s to ensure that every vote is counted, to protect the certification of the election, or to fight back.
* Learn what it means to become a democracy guardian and a critical part of the rapid response to protect our communities.

| [RSVP Now](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96749000/511053088/-1422015579?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4NjZkYjcwLTM3OWQtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=h7O5m3JCgglWvmqlExI0wyc7U7FC7yG1GfVO0MMDRP8=&emci=ca6fd104-889c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=d866db70-379d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=3825287)
---
## 2) Add your name now to let us know you’re ready to fight back with One PA.
In the following days and weeks, we must continue to protect our communities — and our democracy — from far-right extremists and MAGA forces.
_**Our work is far from over.**_
After the 2016 election, millions of Americans channeled their energy into resisting much of the worst of the Trump Administration. We were able to stop the dismantling of the Affordable Care Act, flipped Congress in the midterms, and much, much more. We'll need to resist the Trump Administration once again.
[Sign Up. Fight Back.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96749001/511053089/-1862344868?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4NjZkYjcwLTM3OWQtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=h7O5m3JCgglWvmqlExI0wyc7U7FC7yG1GfVO0MMDRP8=&emci=ca6fd104-889c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=d866db70-379d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=3825287)
---
In the following days and weeks, we’ll continue to be in touch as we learn more. Thank you so much for fighting alongside One Pennsylvania.
We couldn’t do this without you,
Steve Paul
One Pennsylvania
Executive Director

| OnePA is a grassroots movement for racial, economic, and social justice in Pennsylvania. We empower Black communities through voter education, endorse candidates who share our values, and prioritize leadership development. Our commitment is to challenge political disillusionment, protect and expand democracy, and unite everyday people to have a voice in our political system.
---

[Learn More](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96749022/511053107/-501347211?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4NjZkYjcwLTM3OWQtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=h7O5m3JCgglWvmqlExI0wyc7U7FC7yG1GfVO0MMDRP8=&emci=ca6fd104-889c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=d866db70-379d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=3825287) | [Become a Member](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96749025/511053115/-302274394?amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzY2MTk3IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ4NjZkYjcwLTM3OWQtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=h7O5m3JCgglWvmqlExI0wyc7U7FC7yG1GfVO0MMDRP8=&emci=ca6fd104-889c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=d866db70-379d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=3825287)

name: Josh Hawley
subject: Missouri, Thank You!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee
body:
| | | [](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=d1d08772c89049f9ae9a138cff6ad5cf&_e=4b11mUgpSsHcehwn4qA0-9vsFHpF8cVHOPzWj_Ngpqd8KB8tBA5-SEaHMqnYMV2CFSEaXCWSIQZTtOUcfhCB_UKeT5b-eKmTGasZwKfaHX39RU8cvIKBhvZUVfhF5-jc4GYk0r9CFcBMnykFTDY56Qdj2fs3pBJ4lWhp1lvSBsof1p4OkRvG2F5QW5__aHOVrNBszpYnlENu1RnCDGACHHKmefLjDvPya8tVUml7g9ReabO2dAVs7MgGUp6OJl2JeSqiPSLYakk0HVkrorB4g-yKNlW0OH4BHM9hxHn0CSr3G0arylIrBIrV-UD0NmR6NWvQTvf66aug-zScl5TeGAxstqdyxCyG8gOKy_CNCQlpE6A4Jb5WfvjfI_zGbvDl)
---
Peter,

---
From the bottom of my heart, I want to say thank you. Thank you to everyone who knocked on doors, made phone calls, volunteered at events, or donated— I could not have done this without you. Thank you for all that you've done for this country and this state!

On Tuesday, Missouri spoke for the nation by delivering a decisive message — we believe in this country and that God is not done with America. I head back to the United States Senate encouraged and emboldened for what is next. Despite the massive victory on Tuesday, I’m here to say, we’re just getting started! _**WATCH THE CAMPAIGN JOURNEY UNFOLD BELOW⬇︎⬇︎⬇︎**_

name: Dominic Rapini
subject: Call to Action: Ranked Choice Voting Testimony
body:
Cut, past, send, be heard.

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/a880b9b81167/when-we-vote-we-win-14760713?e=8763c56857)
https://www.dominicforct.us

Permission granted to download and use all content in this email for print and digital

** The Next Attack on Connecticut Elections:
------------------------------------------------------------

** Ranked Choice Voting (RCV)
------------------------------------------------------------

Tomorrow morning, the Government Administration and Election Committee will host a public testimony session to consider Ranked Choice Voting. Governor Lamont made a campaign promise to promote this controversial voting system in partnership with the CT Independent Party.

I have compiled a comprehensive white paper on the subject to provide you with more information.

Here’s what you can do: Copy and paste my testimony into your own email and send it to the GAE Committee. Let’s overwhelm the system with our counterarguments and make our voices heard.
Download the Testimony PDF (https://drive.google.com/file/d/1u3ECAtZgyL1fsCVGbgMNWkASCKGLBqS4/view?usp=sharing)

** Send Email Tonight or Tomorrow Morning:
------------------------------------------------------------

** TO: Giovanni.Pinto@ct.gov
------------------------------------------------------------

** Testimony: Ranked Choice Voting is a Threat to the Integrity of Connecticut Elections
------------------------------------------------------------

(Cut and paste into a Word Document, or send a PDF)

One Voter, One Vote.

Connecticut is facing many challenges right now, but the way we vote should not be one of them. Proponents of Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) argue that it will make our elections fairer, more inclusive, and more democratic. But that claim falls apart under scrutiny. RCV is not just a different way to vote—it’s a radical departure from the principle that has been at the foundation of American elections for nearly two and a half centuries: One Voter, One Vote.

In Connecticut, we already have a robust electoral system. Six political parties can appear on the ballot, each with the opportunity to rally support, present their ideas, and make their case to the voters. In a traditional election, candidates compete for a simple, clear-cut victory: the candidate who receives the most votes wins. Every vote is counted with equal weight, and the outcome is decisive. But with RCV, this straightforward process becomes convoluted, diluting the principle of a single, direct vote for a single candidate.

Confusion and Complexity Harm Voters

RCV introduces unnecessary confusion into an already complex electoral process. Instead of casting a single vote, RCV asks voters to rank candidates in order of preference. This sounds simple in theory, but in practice, it burdens voters with the responsibility of evaluating every candidate on the ballot. Most people need help to form strong opinions about more than one or two candidates; asking them to rank a dozen in order of preference is unrealistic and even unfair. Studies show that many voters, unsure how to rank candidates they know little about, will leave these choices blank.

When voters don’t rank every candidate, it leads to a phenomenon known as “ballot exhaustion.” If all the candidates a voter has ranked are eliminated in earlier rounds of counting, that ballot is thrown out—it no longer counts. This effectively disenfranchises voters, silencing their voice in the final outcome of the election. A New York City primary in 2021, conducted with RCV, saw over 140,000 ballots discarded due to ballot exhaustion. That’s 140,000 people who went to the polls expecting their vote to matter—only to be effectively erased from the results.

Delays and Uncertainty Undermine Trust

RCV doesn’t just confuse voters; it also introduces delays and uncertainty into the process. With RCV, elections can require multiple rounds of counting, stretching the process over days or even weeks. In New York City’s 2021 primary, it took three weeks to declare a winner after eight rounds of counting. In Alaska’s 2022 primary, two weeks passed before the final outcome was clear. In an era when trust in elections is already precarious, RCV’s drawn-out process only feeds public skepticism.

An election system that can’t deliver swift, decisive results is a system that risks losing the confidence of the people it serves.

RCV Does Not Reflect the Will of the People

One of the most troubling aspects of RCV is that it can produce winners who lack a clear mandate from the electorate. Under RCV, it’s entirely possible for a candidate who receives the most first-choice votes to lose the election after lower-ranked votes are reallocated. This process allows candidates to win based on second, third, or even fourth-choice rankings rather than on direct support from most voters. The winner of an RCV election might only have a lukewarm plurality of support rather than the solid majority that a traditional election requires.

This undermines the legitimacy of the election. How can a candidate claim a mandate when most voters didn’t choose them as their first choice? RCV ultimately diminishes the power of each vote, muddying the results and diminishing public trust in the winner’s legitimacy.

RCV Is a Solution in Search of a Problem

RCV advocates often frame it as a form of “election reform,” but it’s a complicated fix for a problem that doesn’t exist. Connecticut’s current electoral system isn’t broken. We have a healthy array of candidates, competitive races, and a transparent, efficient voting process. RCV proponents argue that it will make elections “fairer” by giving lesser-known candidates a better shot at winning. But is that really what we want? RCV effectively lowers the bar for winning, allowing candidates with fringe ideas or minimal public support to succeed by siphoning off lower-ranked votes rather than earning genuine, first-choice support from most voters.

Elections are about making hard choices between candidates with competing visions. RCV blurs these choices, making it harder for voters to distinguish between severe contenders and marginal candidates. By allowing candidates to win based on second or third-choice rankings, RCV encourages mediocrity and muddles the distinction between frontrunners and those who don’t have a realistic chance of leading.

The Cost of Complexity Is Voter Disenfranchisement

It’s not just hypothetical: RCV has a proven record of increased ballot spoilage due to voter mistakes. The instructions for ranking candidates are more complicated than traditional voting, leading to more spoiled ballots—ballots that are tossed out because voters made mistakes while navigating this convoluted system. In practical terms, this means more Connecticut voters could see their voices go unheard.

The evidence is clear: RCV is a misguided, convoluted system that disenfranchises voters rather than empowers them. It violates the longstanding principle of One Voter, One Vote, which has guided American elections since the founding of this country. Connecticut’s leaders should focus on the real issues facing our state—not on experimenting with an election system that will make it harder for citizens to understand the process, trust the results, and feel confident that their vote truly counts.

Let’s not trade clarity for confusion, decisiveness for delay, or genuine representation for a false sense of choice. Ranked Choice Voting is not the way forward for Connecticut. It’s time to put this misguided experiment to rest and recommit to our traditional voting system's simplicity, integrity, and fairness.

Dominic Rapini, Branford, CT
Download the Testimony PDF (https://drive.google.com/file/d/1u3ECAtZgyL1fsCVGbgMNWkASCKGLBqS4/view?usp=sharing)

clean_body: | | | It’s James Carville and I’m so damn stressed I can hardly type this message! Jacky Rosen’s Senate race is STILL coming down to the wire - and we’re running out of time. Jacky’s team is hustling to make sure every vote is counted fairly, but her campaign just let me know they’re seriously behind their emergency fundraising goal – and they might have to STOP their operation if she can’t catch up. We’re letting Nevada slip through our fingers, and I need you to LOCK. IN. RIGHT. NOW. I’ve seen this film before. Nevada’s last Senate race was decided by less than 5 votes per precinct – and we ONLY won because we rolled up our sleeves and made sure every ballot was counted. But right now, Jacky does NOT have the show of grassroots support she needs to keep her campaign running. We have a path to victory, but it’ll take every last one of us getting off of our you-know-whats and taking action TODAY. That means we cannot get complacent, or this shtshow will become a total CLUSTERF &K. We’re working on borrowed time, my friend. And I don’t want to let all of our hard work here in Nevada go down the drain in the eleventh hour. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Thanks. – James Carville Paid for by Defeat Boebert PAC

name: Eric Sorensen
subject: The people of IL-17 have spoken
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Sorensen for Illinois
body:
[|
---](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/qcYTfGX_rVL0pDDcRPwH3g~~/AAUS1gA~/RgRpD5KiP0QgaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZXJpY2ZvcmlsbGlub2lzLmNvbS9XA3NwY0IKZyymDS1nYfADOVIUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAABs~)
| | We did it, Peter. The people of IL-17 have spoken—and they’re sending their neighbor back to Congress! Thank you, Central and Northwestern Illinois, for your trust and support. And thank you to each and every one of you who’s been by our side in this re-election campaign. To the volunteers, donors, staff members, and our incredible friends in organized labor: YOU are the reason we won this race. I also understand if not everything feels like celebrating today, given the presidential result. No matter how you feel this morning, we all must find our strength. Because we have work to do. We will continue to fight for our rights.
We will continue to fight for equality.
We will keep our sights ahead.
We will believe that it gets better.
We will listen to advice.
We will show up to do good work.
We will help others as we go.
We will lead with compassion.
We will walk with grace.
We will speak when we’re wronged.
We will lend our voice to the speechless.
We will continue to learn.
We will believe only facts.
We will remember our mistakes.
We will push harder.
We will love when others hate.
We will care for those who need help.
We will demand our nation be better.
We will believe in our purpose.
We will walk together. And we will move forward with all of the above, not some. Onward, for our democracy. For our rights. For the people. -Eric | **[Donate](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/tAVI85wdOQxyMnRhyfEyBA~~/AAUS1gA~/RgRpD5KiP0TkaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL2VzLXE0LTIwMjQ_)**

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Thank you for being by my side in our shared struggle
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
Together, we will never stop holding the powerful accountable in our struggle for human dignity for all people. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWB5gq-DgAwoZcyRShke9qP1AMc_lVD-pBj6ENKZ2slhJjFPijSKu3-LmrMAJrzKrZxbuSgRVKomlcYV-vXhAblrjswN-9hmikrCUeGJpWIOCIMZ3YzwlunDLM2FW3eDDIDsxIjN3oxwtCTM-C_aKn8sqB8tfG2OIsE5micOwSQiwIZqeR6goMRLBVWBWFkDEyMI7vPc9cw-qFZ54qo88gg5QZh03FFnNBdhsSBEU9nd0/4b9/7TY-rmWkQ06FypgqbVaynA/h0/h001.7RmETDjQqK3_ploFUQutcf2cEcpEO8hRhwss-9ay1NM)
---
Friend, From the bottom of my heart, thank you for believing in me and sending me back to Congress. Thanks to you and the hard work of so many volunteers, donors, and members of our campaign team, I am honored and humbled to be heading back to the People’s House to continue to represent you as we fight for economic, racial, social, and environmental justice. As you’ve seen, Donald Trump will again be president. I pushed to hold him accountable before getting into Congress, and once I was elected, I led on impeaching Trump from day one. Unfortunately, he was able to secure a far-right majority on the U.S. Supreme Court, taking away our rights such as the nationwide right to abortion. A second Trump term will be disastrous for our communities, and we need to push President Biden to do as much as possible in his final months in office. I want you to know that I will be standing alongside all of you as we fight to mitigate the harm Trump will cause to our Black, brown, LGBTQ+, immigrant, and working class neighbors. Together, we will never stop holding the powerful accountable in our struggle for human dignity for all people—including ending the genocide of the Palestinian people. When I asked my yama (mom) why she voted, she said: “Because they can’t erase us.” No matter what is ahead, we won’t let those in power silence us and ignore the needs of our communities. Everyday people have felt like our government is not listening to their voices, and we will keep fighting for a government that truly represents the people—not the billionaires or corporations. Over the last 7 weeks of the election, our team contacted 40,000 voters in Michigan’s 12th congressional district through door knocking, phone calls, texts, postcards, letters, and events―boosting turnout for Democrats up and down the ballot. On Election Day alone we made 20,000 phone calls. Direct human contact and increasing voter turnout is how we make sure our democracy is stronger and made up of all of us, and we won’t stop directly connecting with our neighbors. I ran for Congress in 2018 to lift up the voices of people in my community. Now I’m heading into my fourth term in Congress to continue representing my neighbors in Michigan’s 12th congressional district. Here are some of our accomplishments so far: Through our Neighborhood Service Centers in the district, our team has now resolved around 9,000 cases for constituents seeking help with veterans’ services, immigration issues, Social Security and disability benefits, securing back pay, and more—bringing more than $6.1 million directly back to our residents. We’ve also secured over $57 million in federal funding for 25 local community projects, including $7 million to replace dangerous lead service lines and support the right to clean water. Between 2019 and 2024, I passed 40 bills and amendments (out of 160 introduced), including legislation to remediate toxic PFAS chemicals, remove medically necessary debt from credit reports, expand homeownership opportunities, increase renewable energy funding, increase suicide prevention for veterans, and provide $23 billion to replace dangerous lead-contaminated water pipes as well as $1.1 billion to stop water shutoffs. The policies we introduce are developed in partnership with our constituents, and as I head into my next term in Congress, I will continue to push for bold changes to end poverty, take on corporate greed, and help working class people thrive, not just survive. As Grace Lee Boggs taught me, change does not begin with the people in power, change begins on the streets when we build multi-racial, multi-faith coalitions. In the past year alone, we raised and donated over $152,000 for grassroots nonprofit organizations―all thanks to folks like you, folks. Thanks to you and other members of our movement, we recently raised over $35,000 for Palestine Legal to support their legal advocacy for people speaking out for Palestine. As repression against activists will likely increase, we will continue to show up for one another and build our movements for progressive change. Thank you for being by my side. Together, we continue the struggle for justice and equity for all. Always in Service, Rashida [DONATE NOW](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtjzbjFBpR7JpBHI31l9kbTssL2iCd_LDl88OA1cO_uPZTh_4qb-dDV4KslVIGGFBvuyyaFVqhBAt39x-ueL4ETixkrokMRagJduoaIQcazkdCbSCvu5ojkJbTeHPEa_7mNwkUQqIjKEG7FPdPuu03PKyzH-WNUtatpv4dFvkXlRjmiw_ebkWZE6r0EF9c4Ok_sWH_Rm1QIQ0So-r4Ou1T8E_UT5I6YwxuGK9ZAJ1ND8sOqVVhUsN4OUreLCK_gEssbHkK6i1-aWHyRyRguYC3hLXu_XkKb1y4BNa60rG-2YBrKTzel77IZqHhKv4-MOBii2GCXLD0ZN67SqzVkOtV9-28zUf9toC7pGJJQKCZPK5pjumxuKkmeeZZLxcbON-yshW9LbTLL4RvRXTfuvgkhP1U4V-vGA0lx-lUo2OTpGfg15717BpCt0PAPjiRnalU/4b9/7TY-rmWkQ06FypgqbVaynA/h1/h001.ZYuqjFwipBJeHXnb2qrrCaVLASZjIe-S_5wYiNSC6_w)

name: Eric Swalwell
subject: Our work is far from over
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://ericswalwell.com/ [https://ericswalwell.com/]
Friends,

Tuesday night wasn’t the outcome we hoped for. We put in the work, believed in the possibility, and fell short this time. It stings. But I want you to know that this is not the end of our fight. Not by a long shot.

Our movement has always been about something bigger than one election. It’s about protecting the promise of America—where democracy is real, voices are heard, and no one is above the law.

Trump and his loyalists are determined to pull us backward to a place where only their interests and power matter. From suppressing voting rights to rewriting the rulebook of basic accountability, their goal is crystal clear: power without consequences. And we simply can’t allow that.

WE WONT GO BACK

This is why we keep going. I know you’re tired, and I know it can feel like an uphill battle. But you give me hope. I see the commitment and strength in this movement and in people like you, and I know we have what it takes to keep going, no matter the obstacles.

So, let’s take a moment to feel this setback but not let it define us. Tomorrow, we will keep organizing. We keep speaking out. And together, we keep pushing for the kind of future we know is possible. Because our democracy is worth it, and so is every person we’re fighting for.

With gratitude and resolve,

Eric

name: Mike Schmuhl
subject: Thanks for all you do
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/67635/images/IDP%20logo.jpg]
Hoosier Democrats,

There's no question that Tuesday's results were a gut punch for our party at the national and state levels. We have a lot more work to do to appeal to Hoosier voters in cities, towns, suburbs, and rural areas.

While most of the results were not what we had hoped, the outcome makes our work as Hoosier Democrats that much more important. We need to hold politicians like Mike Braun, Micah Beckwith, Todd Rokita, and Diego Morales accountable each day as they push extreme policies or do things that hurt the livelihoods of people in our state.

We are entering uncharted waters with a second Trump presidency and what appears to be mostly unchecked power from the White House. We will need a bigger coalition of people in our state to work together to build more Democratic power at every level of government in our state for the future.

Next year, our party infrastructure will reorganize at the county, district, and state levels. In the primary of 2026, people can run for precinct committee person in their neighborhoods. These are just a few options to get involved directly with improving our party's standing in your communities.

Not all of the results on Tuesday were disappointing. U.S. Rep. Frank Mrvan won reelection in northwest Indiana after being targeted by national Republicans. Moreover, we did not lose a single Democratic incumbent in the statehouse -- many other state chambers lost seats.

Please join me in thanking our great statewide ticket -- Jennifer McCormick, Terry Goodin, Valerie McCray, and Destiny Wells -- as well as the people who ran for office at every level. They ran meaningful campaigns and they made us proud.

Lastly, thank you to all of you for being in this work alongside us. Your ideas, volunteerism, humor, criticisms, donations, hard work, and values keep our party going.

Thank you again,

Mike Schmuhl
Chairman

name: Dr. Ben Carson
subject: Get your copy of Why America Matters
body:
Are you worried about the rising influence of radical ideologies in today's schools?

|

[](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=32e8f2c6139b43a496d13204f97bbb2e&_e=z2xFh8R99dgQT4xGCobi616cJwCtT6OcNwZRTHUZYSL2vdnBs4irLpmVPKI7p7w6NAEEAL11s8OvGbon8n8JJPuySXkUhswhC7OETbK5UHgEZZJfh5ocM0HSCDTPs0lq9OcX7iQ0a2WFr9bNXHiFJoz2cvckQbZdkNz8YW5xLfvd3SvdthBI3Ec_X_8RugkPyKIm7GkkE5ETnR604lxaVevNqzNhAzR7tK0kqEvNuMlddgG3lWyjclZYZVxnjAUKY_Ql1B-HAS8-up5YHrnHAGSjLcRjdG1nyR9LINDECn6_1PGV4-4wnBOar_e8oeKLYX119eAmEj6JNoWb2v3pK1xaBcwALXssT3UpVmh7HYzGOWnplVuqy6InRcve-Lr20RETGS8_0IGjTYOataOVNA%3D%3D)
|
Fellow American,

Are you worried about the rising influence of radical ideologies in today's schools? I know I am.

That's why I have created the Little Patriots Program. This program provides curriculum for grades K-5 that teaches the REAL history of America, promoting the values of faith, liberty, community, and life that made our nation great.

This innovative approach makes learning history fun for our children while instilling in them a profound love and respect for our country. [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=32e8f2c6139b43a496d13204f97bbb2e&_e=z2xFh8R99dgQT4xGCobi616cJwCtT6OcNwZRTHUZYSL2vdnBs4irLpmVPKI7p7w6NAEEAL11s8OvGbon8n8JJPuySXkUhswhC7OETbK5UHgEZZJfh5ocM0HSCDTPs0lq9OcX7iQ0a2WFr9bNXHiFJoz2cvckQbZdkNz8YW5xLfvd3SvdthBI3Ec_X_8RugkPyKIm7GkkE5ETnR604lxaVevNqzNhAzR7tK0kqEvNuMlddgG3lWyjclZYZVxnjAUKKO8GIBBS414xlFPo8n36MjCcdfEuD77A4ArtXlombKc1wv31H7MJ4qibUn1Zf3itVg1N5Hvs--fzUQsG9L5jlqrdlz9PFlzgEJD7WmsqLCqrfjyj5N_AB5cpNIj4LtM81iesAThDORGLUfL21PRYDg%3D%3D)
__ But, in order to keep this curriculum **free of charge,** I need your support.

I have written a book, [_Why America Matters_](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=32e8f2c6139b43a496d13204f97bbb2e&_e=z2xFh8R99dgQT4xGCobi616cJwCtT6OcNwZRTHUZYSL2vdnBs4irLpmVPKI7p7w6NAEEAL11s8OvGbon8n8JJPuySXkUhswhC7OETbK5UHgEZZJfh5ocM0HSCDTPs0lq9OcX7iQ0a2WFr9bNXHiFJoz2cvckQbZdkNz8YW5xLfvd3SvdthBI3Ec_X_8RugkPyKIm7GkkE5ETnR604lxaVevNqzNhAzR7tK0kqEvNuMlddgG3lWyjclZYZVxnjAUK47TghIsxOIn0da_VMZXqKKfc_1Rz78zAabSEHFo4qI0S3Z9bh3AdN5zWLTUSTzv7yzarA1BQZvVrvpCoSnX0OFVkw-lSLV7W53TbdZbz9pW7PPNIOcNP6xU5DmbrJH0Zr_p2lIAA-QupzMiwpyQTcw%3D%3D). Intended for young readers, this book creates a deeper understanding of the importance of our country and the respect that it deserves.

I want to send you a copy for a gift of $25 or more. To give a gift use this secure link: [https://secure.americancornerstone.org/why-america-matters-donate](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=32e8f2c6139b43a496d13204f97bbb2e&_e=z2xFh8R99dgQT4xGCobi616cJwCtT6OcNwZRTHUZYSL2vdnBs4irLpmVPKI7p7w6NAEEAL11s8OvGbon8n8JJPuySXkUhswhC7OETbK5UHgEZZJfh5ocM0HSCDTPs0lq9OcX7iQ0a2WFr9bNXHiFJoz2cvckQbZdkNz8YW5xLfvd3SvdthBI3Ec_X_8RugkPyKIm7GkkE5ETnR604lxaVevNqzNhAzR7tK0kqEvNuMlddgG3lWyjclZYZVxnjAUK8Sd659KMwDi4Ulc9qQSRKlkylWmDT6SNN4Tw_fBLyq81OP7AOssau_vj69Alnpxocz2-tpDPZYtopbRE1SlykZe3sbgqq85Xk9VNat-Dqy3MB0WQXptqOvhBSU-q0_BbSPidyR9XLUMjzdoq_qhrxQ%3D%3D)

All proceeds from your donation will go directly to supporting our Little Patriots Program, ensuring that we can keep creating curricula and cartoons that show the history of America.

You can help shape the minds of our future leaders today and give them the best possible resources.

Your gift of $25 or more will get you access to my book, _Why America Matters_ , and help us reach more children with the truth.

Sincerely,

Dr. Ben Carson
Founder and Chairman
American Cornerstone Institute

name: Costello for Senate
subject: Thank you!
body:
| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/e26719ab287f/thank-you?e=5d7176e969)
---
| | | | |
# Thank you!
Dear Team Costello,Thank you very, very much for your help and support, because of it we were able to mount a substantive, reform and public policy focused campaign that exceeded expectations. When the final vote is tallied it looks as though we’ll end up having garnered more than 85,000 votes (roughly 11%) in the four-person race. A race that pitted us against a popular establishment supported incumbent and GOP nominee who respectively outspent us 200 and 40 to 1.
In view of this, we have much to be proud of and given the monumentally disappointing results of our national presidential and congressional elections, we have a lot more work to do. Our efforts to substantially reform our governing practices, programs, and institutions by enacting long overdue legislative and constitutional reforms are now more important than ever! So, to borrow from Senator Edward Kennedy’s excellent August 1980 convention speech, our “work goes on, the cause endures, the hope still lives, and the dream [of a more democratic and perfect union] shall never die.”
Best always,David [Costellodavida37@gmail.com](mailto:Costellodavida37@gmail.com) (going forward)

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: I'm personally asking for your help
body:
Folks, I wouldn't reach out again if it weren't important.
Our race in Nevada isn’t over. This race will be one of the tightest in the country, and unless we raise another $115,762 before midnight, we could fall short of the critical resources we need to win.
The hard truth is that Nevada is won by the margins, so please, will you chip in right now to help tip the scales in our favor to keep Nevada blue?
Here's the best link to split a donation between my campaign and The Real Texas PAC: secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-rosen [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-rosen?refcode=110724_2]
Best,
Jacky
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-august-2024?refcode=110724_2 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-august-2024?refcode=110724_2]
The Real Texas PAC
1150 N Loop 1604 W
Ste 108-230
San Antonio, TX 78248
United States
Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. This race will be one of the tightest in the country, and unless we raise another $115,762 before midnight, we could fall short of the critical resources we need to win. The hard truth is that Nevada is won by the margins, so please, will you chip in right now to help tip the scales in our favor to keep Nevada blue? name: Andrea Salinas
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96750427/511058427/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyNTFjNWM5Yi0zYjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YS27Qsqc6HftjpFzt6FEl_xrk5vP8y4dw9XaDi57ZDU=) I wanted to get in touch today to thank you for everything you did to make our victory here in OR-06 possible — even as that victory is turning out to be a bittersweet one. I ran for Congress because I saw too many working families following all the rules and still falling short. Because I saw my daughter’s generation losing the freedoms that we had fought so hard to protect. **It is not an easy fight, and I wouldn’t be here without the incredible team that helped me get here. And I want you to know that I’m going back to D.C. ready to fight like hell.** Now is the time to stay strong and stay focused on the work ahead of us. The road may be long, and we will face obstacles along the way – but that doesn’t mean we give up. Democrats don’t give up. We push forward, united by our shared convictions and the promise of a better tomorrow. Thank you for believing in me and in our campaign. No matter where we go from here, remember that we’re in this fight together. I promise to continue being a champion for Oregon and Oregon values. And I will never back down. And I ask that you don’t either. With gratitude, Andrea

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: We’re not done fighting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tab5599f0-4ab8-4874-a7d8-d23de2bed25e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Peter, Tuesday’s results were beyond disappointing. It’s disheartening to know a majority of our nation chose to reelect an amoral con man whose inaction during the COVID-19 pandemic cost the lives of over a million Americans. It’s unfathomable that Trump’s decades of racist and sexist remarks – and felony convictions! – did not stop him from winning the White House again. Kamala Harris deserved this win. She ran a near-perfect campaign and has decades of experience advocating for policies that improve lives for all Americans, not just those with lots of money. Also, her class, her wish to unite us all, was what America needed at this moment. I am joining tens of millions of Americans in mourning what we’ve lost by not electing her. I am, however, trying to remain hopeful via the victories we did have. Lisa Blunt Rochester and Angela Alsobrooks will make history as the fourth and fifth Black women to ever serve in the Senate – and it will be the first time America has two Black women in the Senate at the same time. Andy Kim’s win in New Jersey means we’ll have the first Korean-American Senator. And Delaware’s Sarah McBride is going to be America’s first openly transgender Congressperson. Through this dark time, it’s okay to mourn and grieve – but let’s also remember the good that is worth fighting for. We must unite more than ever before to defend LGBTQ+ and reproductive rights, protect our climate, and prevent Trump from implementing his dangerous Project 2025 agenda. [I hope you’re still with me in this fight, Peter. Hi co-pilot, We turned a dark page in this chapter of America, and Brian Fitzpatrick has a responsibility to stand up for our democracy and the hundreds of thousands of women he represents to protect our freedoms. As a combat veteran who put my life on the line for our country, I will always fight for our democracy and to secure equal rights – for all of us. To everyone who donated, volunteered, and demanded better for the families of Bucks and MontCo, I’ll say one more time: **Thanks for being in this fight with me as my co-pilot.** Ashley 🚁

name: Gabe Vasquez
subject: Thank you, New Mexico.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress
body:
Saludos, it's Gabe. I just wanted to say thank you.

On Tuesday, thanks to you, we held this district blue.

Serving New Mexico's second congressional district is a privilege and honor, and I will continue to work for the people and families of this district and this state. I am grateful for the trust that our voters have placed in me and pledge to help every constituent that I represent.

It is that devotion and love of country that drives me to do this work. Thank you, New Mexico, for the opportunity to serve you again. Si se puede, si se pudo — y ahora — regresamos a trabajar. Que viva el segundo distrito de Nuevo Mexico!

— Gabe Vasquez

---
# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Reader,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.

subject: This is urgent
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
I'm personally asking for your help
| | |
---
Friend, I wouldn't reach out again if it weren't important.
We are on the verge of winning this U.S. Senate race in Nevada, but it’s going to take making sure that every ballot is counted. Unless we raise another $115,762 before midnight, we could fall short of the critical resources we need to win this.

The hard truth is that Nevada is won by the margins, so please, will you chip in right now to help tip the scales in our favor to keep Nevada blue and protect our Senate majority?

name: Mazie Hirono
subject: Mahalo, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Aloha Peter, Mahalo for standing with me in my successful reelection to the U.S. Senate. It’s because of supporters like you who invested in our grassroots campaign, stayed the course with me, and stepped up during every big moment that I will be able to continue the fight in the U.S. Senate. Because we will need to continue the fight. I have always said that the battles we previously thought we won, don’t stay won. With the gutting news that Donald Trump has won reelection and Republicans have gained control of the Senate, this is especially true now. I will always support the constitution, and the rule of law. I will never stop fighting for the people of Hawaii, and all those who share the values we hold dear — diversity, respect, and the spirit of aloha. Thank you again for standing with me, and for all that you do. — Mazie
Friends of Mazie Hirono
335 Merchant Street
#677
Honolulu, HI 96809
United States **[info@mazieforhawaii.com](mailto:info@mazieforhawaii.com)** | **Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
---

clean_body: Peter, Here’s what we know: This race is not over, and we still have work to do. If we’re going to send Bob Casey back to the Senate and hold the line for our rights and freedoms, we need your immediate support to ensure that every last vote in this PA Senate race is counted. Thank you, Team Casey

name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: We're headed to a recount…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
We won't stop until every vote is counted.

| Peter,

I know many of us are still processing the results of Tuesday's election and what it means for our district and our nation. Donald Trump's victory presents challenges we must now confront together, and we'll need a strong presence in the People's House of Representatives to find the Big Middle, breaking down partisan divides.

Results are still coming in, and it looks like our race may result in a recount despite our small lead. That's why we must do everything we can to win the House or limit the GOP majority - that means holding the line in OH-09.

Peter, I need your support to fund our recount efforts and help us get over the finish line. Can I count on you right now?

**Here's the link:[_https://secure.actblue.com/donate/recount_em_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96751063/511061005/653402453?refcode=2411_mk_recount_partners&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjExYzcxZTE3LTNmOWQtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=kOGpkXPQtckkWlvJdB3vch79gZIIX5Ab5rPNgDlh3vY=)**

subject: The Silent Majority is silent no longer

body:
We’re sending President Trump a HUGE thank you note and we want you to sign it!

|
---
| | | **THANK YOU PRESIDENT TRUMP!

FROM: Friend & House GOP**
---
| | [READ & SIGN LETTER TO TRUMP](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/UcdZNdNuv4gdIxgb6dJ8kg~~/AARclAA~~~)
---
We're putting together a wonderful Letter to President Trump to say thank you for all he's done for House Republicans and the American people!

This letter was written from the heart, and it is the culmination of our months of hard work as we fought tooth and nail to return President Trump to the White House.

And thankfully, President Trump will lead our nation once again.

_**[Sign our letter and say THANK YOU to President Trump!>>>](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/UcdZNdNuv4gdIxgb6dJ8kg~~/AARclAA~~~ "Sign our letter and say THANK YOU to President Trump! >>>")**_
_**THANK YOU PRESIDENT TRUMP!**_
---
| | [READ & SIGN LETTER TO TRUMP](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/UcdZNdNuv4gdIxgb6dJ8kg~~/AARclAA~~~)
---
| [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/UcdZNdNuv4gdIxgb6dJ8kg~~/AARclAA~~~)
---
He was put to the test in this tough presidential race- but just as the phoenix rises from the ashes, so does President Trump emerge victorious and ready to lead.

And for that, he deserves our prayers and a HUGE thank you! **_[Will you sign House Republicans thank you note to President Trump to say thank you for all he's done for our country?](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/UcdZNdNuv4gdIxgb6dJ8kg~~/AARclAA~~~ "Will you sign House Republicans thank you note to President Trump to say thank you for all he’s done for our country?")_**
